title: The Problem with AI Agents No One is Talking About | Yutori, Abhishek Das
summary: Abhishek Das argues that most AI agents fail in practice because error compounding across multi-step workflows is unavoidable at typical accuracy levels. He contends that the industry has normalized unreliability and that shipping working agents requires prioritizing reliability over raw performance, taste in design, and systematic dogfooding.
